source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/10_자사 품질 매뉴얼, 절차서/영문본/☆ 품질 매뉴얼 및 ISO 절차서 (영문본)/원본/ISO Procedures EN/AQP713 Manufacture Process Validation Regulation/AQP713_Manufacture Process Validation Regulation.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Internal controlled regulation defining methods and procedures for manufacturing process validation.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to establish process validation methods and procedures so processes, facilities, or equipment function as intended and consistently produce products.
keyPoints: Process validation applies to methods and procedures for process validation.
Qualification protocols cover IQ, OQ, and PQ stages.
Special processes or automated processes involving computer software must be validated before implementation.
Manufacturing team managers must retain protocols and reports for at least 10 years.
Revalidation must be performed regularly at least once a year and after specified process or product changes.

entities.name: Quality Management Team
entities.evidence: Written Jeong Gyeong gyun Quality Management Team 2024.04.01.

entities.name: Manufacturing Team Leader
entities.evidence: Manufacturing Team Leader Approval of protocol and report Process management

entities.name: Validation Manager
entities.evidence: Validation Manager Preparation of protocols and reports

entities.name: FDA
entities.evidence: Comprehensive revision to comply with KGMP, EN ISO 13485:2016+A11:2021, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and MDR (EU) 2017/745 Article 10 requirements

entities.name: KGMP
entities.evidence: Amendments in accordance with KGMP application

concepts.name: Process Validation
concepts.evidence: The activity of demonstrating with objective evidence that a process consistently produces products or results that meet predetermined requirements

concepts.name: Special process
concepts.evidence: Processes whose results cannot be sufficiently verified by subsequent product inspection and testing, such as when process defects appear only after the product is used

concepts.name: Protocol
concepts.evidence: A document specifying the validation method, including test parameters, product characteristics, manufacturing equipment, and criteria for producing satisfactory test results

concepts.name: Installation qualification, IQ
concepts.evidence: Objective evidence that all significant aspects of the installation of the process equipment and its ancillary systems conform to the manufacturer's approved specifications

concepts.name: Operational qualification, OQ
concepts.evidence: Objective evidence that the process control limits and execution steps affecting the product are consistent with all predetermined requirements

concepts.name: Performance qualification, PQ
concepts.evidence: Objective evidence that a process, performed under expected conditions, consistently produces a product that meets all predetermined requirements.

concepts.name: Revalidation
concepts.evidence: Revalidation must be performed in the following cases.

concepts.name: Recordkeeping
concepts.evidence: Manufacturing team managers must retain protocols and reports for at least 10 years.

evidence.claim: The document was written and approved on 2024.04.01.
evidence.evidenceText: Written Jeong Gyeong gyun Quality Management Team 2024.04.01. ... Approved Hur Nam kyu Quality Management Team 2024.04.01.

evidence.claim: Revision 5 changed the document name and number from AQP711 to AQP713.
evidence.evidenceText: Change in document name and number Process Validation Management Regulation (AQP711) → Manufacturing Process Validation Regulation (AQP713)

evidence.claim: Revision 5 was made to comply with KGMP, EN ISO 13485:2016+A11:2021, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and MDR (EU) 2017/745 Article 10 requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: Comprehensive revision to comply with KGMP, EN ISO 13485:2016+A11:2021, FDA 21 CFR Part 820, and MDR (EU) 2017/745 Article 10 requirements

evidence.claim: The purpose is to establish methods and procedures ensuring processes, facilities, or equipment function as intended and consistently produce products.
evidence.evidenceText: The purpose of this regulation is to establish methods and procedures to ensure that processes, facilities, or equipment function properly as intended and to ensure that consistent products are produced.

evidence.claim: The regulation applies to methods and procedures for process validation.
evidence.evidenceText: This regulation applies to the methods and procedures for process validation.

evidence.claim: IQ, OQ, and PQ qualification protocols must be developed based on process or product requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: A protocol for three stages of qualification—IQ, OQ, and PQ—must be developed and based on the following process or product requirements.

evidence.claim: Special processes or automated processes involving computer software must be validated before implementation.
evidence.evidenceText: Any (automated) process involving special processes or computer software must be validated prior to implementation.

evidence.claim: The validation manager must develop a validation protocol and obtain manufacturing team leader approval.
evidence.evidenceText: The validation manager must develop a validation protocol containing the following details and obtain approval from the manufacturing team leader.

evidence.claim: The process must be implemented after approval of the protocol and report by the manufacturing team leader.
evidence.evidenceText: The process must be implemented after the manufacturing team leader approves the protocol and report.

evidence.claim: Revalidation is required for changes in process variables, changes in product performance or effectiveness, validation results exceeding specified limits, and at least annually.
evidence.evidenceText: Revalidation must be performed in the following cases. ① Changes in process variables ... ② Changes in product performance or effectiveness ③ When validation results exceed specified limits ④ Regularly, at least once a year

evidence.claim: Workers and manufacturing managers must continuously monitor and control process variables and manage records under AQP404.
evidence.evidenceText: Workers and manufacturing managers must continuously monitor and control process variables, record work logs, workers, applied documents, major equipment used, and monitoring data, and manage them in accordance with the Standard Document and Record Management Regulations (AQP404).

evidence.claim: Manufacturing team managers must retain protocols and reports for at least 10 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturing team managers must retain protocols and reports for at least 10 years.

reviewFlags.reason: No product models are identified in the source text.
